_meta.description: Exhaustive reference of Indian military, paramilitary, and police unit/formation names and abbreviations for entity extraction from WhatsApp messages
_meta.usage: Build regex patterns for detecting military unit references in informal text

arms_and_services._doc: Indian Army Arms and Services with abbreviations

eme.full: Corps of Electronics and Mechanical Engineers

infantry_regiment_names._doc: Named regiments of Indian Army infantry and their abbreviations

station_and_hq_types._doc: Station, area, and HQ naming patterns

army_commands._doc: Indian Army Commands relevant for NE India context

ne_india_specific._doc: Units and formations specifically relevant to North East India / Guwahati

examples: HQ DG BSF = Headquarters Director General BSF

officer_titles_army._doc: Army officer and JCO/NCO designations as used in informal text

combined_unit_examples._doc: Real-world examples of how units appear in text — use these to validate patterns

informal_text_patterns._doc: Patterns for how military units appear in informal WhatsApp messages

examples: 107 bde wala order = order from 107 Brigade
stn hq ka order = Station HQ's order
jak rif ke liye = for JAK Rifles
20 jak ka co = CO of 20 JAK Rif
army canteen se = from army canteen
mh ka supply = Military Hospital supply

patterns: Numbers before unit names: '20 jak', '107 bde', '3 fd regt'
All lowercase common: 'stn hq', 'jak rif', 'fd wk sp'
Unit + location: '107 bde guwahati', 'stn hq narangi'
Dropped words: '20 jak' instead of '20 jak rif' or '20th bn jak rif'
Supply context: 'indent from 107 bde', 'order for mh', 'supply to fd wk sp'
Assamese context: 'ar' means Assam Rifles in NE, could be ambiguous

ne_india_locations._doc: Key military stations/cantonments in North East India relevant for Guwahati-based supply business

regex_patterns._doc: Ready-to-use regex patterns for entity extraction (Python re syntax, case-insensitive)

flat_lists_for_quick_lookup._doc: Flat deduplicated lists for quick matching

_meta.description: Actual numbered military units with known details. Use for entity extraction and disambiguation.
_meta.completeness: Best-effort from open sources. Partial info included where full details unavailable.

infantry_regiment_battalions._doc: Known battalion counts per infantry regiment. Individual battalions are numbered 1/Regt, 2/Regt etc.

5_gorkha_rifles.note: 5 GR (Frontier Force), centre at Shillong—significant NE presence

11_gorkha_rifles.note: Raised post-independence, TA bn is 107 Inf Bn (11 GR)

jak_rif.note: Multiple battalions deployed in NE India for CI ops

para_regt.note: Includes SF and Maroon Beret battalions

arunachal_scouts.note: Affiliated to Assam Regiment, based in Arunachal Pradesh

other_india_units.note: Switched from NE CI to Kargil high altitude

bsf_ne.description: BSF guards Indo-Bangladesh border in NE: Assam, West Bengal, Meghalaya, Tripura, Mizoram

ne_frontiers.name: Mizoram & Cachar Frontier
ne_frontiers.border_coverage: Indo-Bangladesh/Myanmar border in Mizoram and Cachar

bsf_ne.note: Specific battalion numbers in NE are operational deployment details; battalions rotate. Total 802 BOPs along 4096 km Indo-Bangladesh border.

crpf_ne.description: CRPF North East Zone handles CI/IS ops in all NE states

itbp_ne.description: ITBP guards India-China border from Karakoram to Diphu La
itbp_ne.ne_deployment: Replaced Assam Rifles in Sikkim and Arunachal Pradesh in 2004. Multiple battalions along LAC in Arunachal.
itbp_ne.recent_additions: 6 new battalions added for Arunachal Pradesh deployment

ssb_ne.description: SSB guards India-Bhutan and India-Nepal borders
ssb_ne.ne_deployment: Guards India-Bhutan border in Assam, West Bengal, Arunachal Pradesh, Sikkim
ssb_ne.historical_note: First established at Tezpur in Assam (1963) after 1962 war

sub_areas.note: Controls military stations in Assam area
Details limited; sub areas are static formations controlling stations

station_hqs.name: Stn HQ Dinjan
station_hqs.note: Nerve centre for LAC operations, operates Heron UAVs
